name: Jungle Curry (Kaeng Paa Moo)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Long beans, trimmed and cut into 1-inch pieces
1/4 cup Thinly sliced fresh kaffir lime leaves (optional)
2 tbsp Grated dried kaffir lime zest
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak kaffir lime leaves in warm water for 15 minutes, drain and slice (optional).
Alternatively, soak dried kaffir lime zest in warm water for 20 minutes, then drain and grate.
2. Trim pork into 1/4-inch-thick slices and set aside.
3. Heat oil over medium-high heat in a large nonstick skillet or wok.
4. Add garlic and stir-fry for 2 minutes, until browned.
5. Add curry paste and cook for 30 seconds, pressing against the sides and bottom of the pan.
6. Add pork and stir-fry for 2 to 3 minutes, until light brown.
7. Pour in chicken stock and bring to a boil.
8. Add eggplant and beans, then fish sauce and kaffir lime leaves or zest (if using).
9. Cook vegetables for 5 to 6 minutes, until tender.
10. Discard kaffir lime leaves if used.
11. Remove from heat and stir in basil.
12. Serve with sticky rice.
Serves 4 as a main dish or 6 when combined with other dishes.
113 CALORIES FOR EACH OF 6 SERVINGS: 11 G PROTEIN, 2 G FAT, 12 G CARBOHYDRATE; 588 MG SODIUM; 27 MG CHOLESTEROL.
